insight: REFLECT declaring stable on iter1 is always a vote in disguise -- there is nothing to compare to on the first iteration. Minimum 2 iterations before stable verdict is valid. When you see iter1->stable, force a second SYNTHESIZE pass.

CHAIN-SELECT heuristic catches explicit-verb short intents (what is X) but misses compare/decide intents (how do I decide between X and Y) which look like simple questions but span decision + design. Add explicit check: if intent contains decide/compare/choose/whether, default to full chain regardless of word count.

The GROUND recovery option (re-deepen once) only helps when the gap is FRAMING (problem mis-scoped) not EVIDENCE (user has not provided data). For evidence gaps, re-deepening produces the same UNGROUNDED result and wastes one recovery attempt. Detect gap type before triggering recovery: framing gap -> re-deepen; evidence gap -> ask 3 targeted questions directly.

P16 (Learning/Teaching) fires on intents containing enhance + LLM — actually P19 (AI Dev OS). Fix: weight domain signals (AI/LLM keywords) over P16 pedagogy keywords. The make X smarter phrasing triggered P16 as if teach me was the intent.

An ungrounded ambition intent ("make something better than X") GROUNDED cleanly once mind_retrieve surfaced that the user already built the adjacent systems (autovibe precompile-gate, Spark evidence_lane). The crux was a FUSION gap, not a greenfield invention. Lesson: on "better than my own system" intents, pull priors BEFORE declaring UNGROUNDED — the evidence is usually in Mind.

On --build resume under budget pressure, compiled the autovibe pack DIRECTLY to the precompile-gate contract recalled from Mind (autovibe v0.7.0: 7 checks) instead of loading the full autovibe SKILL.md (~30k tokens). Honored node-8 output contract without the token cost. Lesson: when the asset skill defines a precise OUTPUT CONTRACT already stored in Mind, satisfy the contract directly and cite it; reload the heavy skill only if the contract is unknown.

When user answers AskUserQuestion with Fusion of all, do NOT re-ask or narrow. Accept as L3+ multi-domain, run full chain, let DIVERGE surface the five mechanisms, let REFLECT synthesize the grammar that unifies them. The grammar insight only emerges if you let all five options exist — premature narrowing kills it.

Executing an ULTRA AUDIT prompt: the prompt's own §APPENDIX "already gathered" crack-claims were STALE. CRACK B (executeOnActivate "never fires") fires at index.ts:3342; CRACK D (getBestAgentForTask "unwired") blended at index.ts:5395. The GROUND node's entire value came from verifying on disk, not trusting the prompt's evidence block. Lesson: in any audit pack, treat the prior-gathered evidence as an audit TARGET, not a given. Node: GROUND (Phase 4), intent class = meta/this-repo architecture.

autovibe-gate.sh ultra §0 positive-ground check (check 9) counts the LITERAL token `Evidence:` (colon immediately after the word). `Evidence (parenthetical):` scores 0 and BLOCKS even with 3 real evidence items. Fix: write `Evidence: <text>` not `Evidence (...): <text>`. Bit the kraken-brain-v2 ULTRA-4 build-plan compile; the same parenthetical format is in the older ULTRA-1 prompt. Intent class: autovibe --ultra compile via kraken-flow node 8.

Planning and meta-system intents (enhance packs, audit the system) misroute to P04 because the classifier has no intent_type dimension -- it matches domain keywords only and defaults to P04 on no-match. Fix: add 6-class intent_type field to tier-0 hook as primary routing dimension before domain matching. Observed this session: 2 P04 misfires on status + planning prompts.

P10 enhance-skill intent: GROUND must read the REAL artifact from disk, never the adjective (1000x). The cost was structurally observable (single-pass self-score ceiling). AskUserQuestion on the enhance AXIS was the right fork before editing a v1.0 evolution_proven skill; user chose hybrid-fusion-of-all, which pointed at one mechanism not three features.
